description: Use this API to return complex bus parameters as MVA. Values may be either nominal or actual. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr, rval = busdt1(ibus, string1, string2)

parameters.name: string1
parameters.description: String indicating the bus quantity desired. (input).

parameters.name: string2
parameters.description: String indicating the units to be used (ignored if string1 = ‘ysz’ or ‘yswz’). (input).

return_values.name: rval
return_values.description: Magnitude of the complex value indicated by string1 in the units indicated by string2. (output).

string1.description: Total in-service scalable constant MVA load.
Total in-service scalable constant current load.
Total in-service scalable constant admittance load.
Total in-service fixed constant MVA load.
Total in-service fixed constant current load.
Total in-service fixed constant admittance load.
Total nominal in-service zero sequence fixed bus shunt.
Total nominal in-service zero sequence fixed bus shunt in per unit.
Total in-service negative sequence load (pu nominal).
Total in-service zero sequence load (pu nominal).

string2.description: Actual load or shunt (nominal shunt for STRING1 = ‘YSZERO’ or ‘YSWZERO’). ‘RVAL’ returned in MVA.
Actual load or shunt (nominal shunt for STRING1 = ‘YSZERO’ or ‘YSWZERO’). ‘RVAL’ in units determined by the power output option setting (units determined by power output options).
Nominal load or shunt (at 1.0 pu voltage). ‘RVAL’ returned in MVA.
Nominal load or shunt (at 1.0 pu voltage). ‘RVAL’ in units determined by the power output option setting.

error_codes.description: Bus type code 4 or greater; ‘RVAL’ returned.
No loads at bus for ‘STRING1’ = ‘MVA’, ‘IL’, ‘YL’, or ‘TOTAL’; ‘RVAL’ returned set to zero.
No switched shunt at bus for ‘STRING1’ = ‘YSW’; ‘YSWZERO’ or ‘YSWZ’; ‘RVAL’ unchanged.
Sequence data not in case for ‘STRING1’ = ‘YSZERO’, ‘YSZ’, ‘YSWZERO’, ‘YSWZ’, ‘SHUNTN’ or ‘SHUNTZ’; ‘RVAL’ unchanged.
